summary: East Africa's commercial and tech hub, a stable democracy and Western security partner whose mobile-money revolution and Nairobi 'Silicon Savannah' coexist with heavy Chinese-built debt.

history.detail: Armed revolt against British colonial rule met with brutal suppression.
Jomo Kenyatta becomes the first leader of an independent Kenya.
Disputed election sparks ethnic clashes killing over 1,000.
Mobile money transforms finance and inspires the world.
Al-Shabaab kills 67; cross-border conflict with Somalia intensifies.
Mass youth protests force the withdrawal of a finance bill.

finance.currency_role: Kenyan shilling (KES); East Africa's reference currency
leverage_tools: Mombasa port — gateway for East Africa
Nairobi as a regional HQ for UN and multinationals

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: One of Africa's top startup markets; fintech and climate tech

resources.oil_gas: Discoveries in Turkana; not yet a major exporter
resources.rare_earths: Titanium, soda ash, rare-earth deposits at Mrima Hill
resources.water: Variable; recurrent drought in the north and east
resources.agriculture: Tea, coffee, horticulture (cut flowers) — top exports

follow_the_money.detail: Mobile money handles a huge share of GDP and financial inclusion
Chinese-financed Standard Gauge Railway is a major debt and political issue
Agricultural exports to Europe and Asia are key FX earners

theories_disputed.detail: Concerns the Mombasa–Nairobi railway loan risks Chinese seizure of the port; both governments deny it.
President Kenyatta and Ruto were charged over 2007 violence; cases later collapsed.

connections.detail: Built the SGR railway and major roads under Belt and Road
Major non-NATO ally; counter-terror cooperation in Somalia
Maritime border dispute and conflict with Al-Shabaab
